verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is synthesized as a type I single-pass transmembrane proform (~19 kDa) with its EGF-like ectodomain exposed on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. The transmembrane pro-form is stably surface-accessible by its own TM anchor prior to ADAM-mediated ectodomain shedding. Multiple EREG-targeted ADC programs confirm antibody binding, internalization, and tumor-selective uptake of the surface-tethered proform. However, surface expression is low in normal tissues and markedly upregulated in colorectal cancer, inflammatory states, and specific carcinomas (bladder, lung, kidney, colon), making this a cell-state/tissue-restricted surface presentation rather than ubiquitous baseline surface expression.
